%% Helper function to compute left maximums for a list.
%% LMaxs[i] stores the maximum of elements nums[0] through nums[i-1].
%% Assumes all input elements are >= 1. We use 0 as the sentinel for empty prefixes.
compute_left_maxes(Nums) ->
    compute_left_maxes_helper(Nums, 0, []).

compute_left_maxes_helper([], _, Acc) ->
    lists:reverse(Acc);
compute_left_maxes_helper([H | T], CurrentMax, Acc) ->
    NewMax = max(CurrentMax, H),
    Acc' = [CurrentMax | Acc],
    compute_left_maxes_helper(T, NewMax, Acc').

%% Helper function to compute right maximums.
%% This is done by reversing the list, computing left maximums, and then reversing the result back.
compute_right_maxes(Nums) ->
    ReversedNums = lists:reverse(Nums),
    LMaxs_Reversed = compute_left_maxes(ReversedNums),
    lists:reverse(LMaxs_Reversed).

%% Main function implementation
solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    case N of
        0 -> [];
        1 -> Nums;
        _ ->
            LMaxs = compute_left_maxes(Nums),
            RMaxs = compute_right_maxes(Nums),
            
            % Iterate through all elements using recursion and the precalculated max arrays
            solve_helper(Nums, LMaxs, RMaxs, []).

% Recursive helper to check validity
solve_helper([], _, _, Acc) ->
    lists:reverse(Acc);
solve_helper([H | T], [LMax | TLMaxs], [RMax | TRMaxs], Acc) ->
    Index = length(Nums) - length([H | T]), % Calculate current index i
    
    % Check boundary conditions (first or last element)
    IsFirst = Index = 0,
    IsLast = Index = length(Nums) - 1,

    % Check core conditions for middle elements
    IsGreaterThanLeft = H > LMax,
    IsGreaterThanRight = H > RMax,
    
    IsValid = IsFirst ; IsLast ; (IsGreaterThanLeft ; IsGreaterThanRight),
    
    NewAcc = if IsValid -> [H | Acc] ; Acc,
    
    solve_helper(T, TLMaxs, TRMaxs, NewAcc).

% We need to handle the length context for solve_helper to work correctly, 
% as length(Nums) is not available inside the helper scope unless passed or redefined.
% Let's restructure the iteration using a loop equivalent where N is known.

solve(Nums) when is_list(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    case N of
        0 -> [];
        1 -> Nums;
        _ ->

            RMaxs = compute_right_maxes(Nums),

            valid_elements =
                lists:foldl(fun(Acc, {i, H}) ->
                    Index = i,
                    
                    IsFirst = Index = 0,
                    IsLast = Index = N - 1,

                    RMax_i = RMaxs !! Index,

%% Helper function to compute left maximums for a list.
%% LMaxs[i] stores the maximum of elements nums[0] through nums[i-1].
%% Assumes all input elements are >= 1. We use 0 as the sentinel for empty prefixes.
compute_left_maxes(Nums) ->
    compute_left_maxes_helper(Nums, 0, []).

compute_left_maxes_helper([], _, Acc) ->
    lists:reverse(Acc);
compute_left_maxes_helper([H | T], CurrentMax, Acc) ->
    NewMax = max(CurrentMax, H),
    Acc' = [CurrentMax | Acc],
    compute_left_maxes_helper(T, NewMax, Acc').

%% Helper function to compute right maximums.
%% This is done by reversing the list, computing left maximums, and then reversing the result back.
compute_right_maxes(Nums) ->
    ReversedNums = lists:reverse(Nums),
    LMaxs_Reversed = compute_left_maxes(ReversedNums),
    lists:reverse(LMaxs_Reversed).

%% Main function implementation
solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    case N of
        0 -> [];
        1 -> Nums;
        _ ->
            LMaxs = compute_left_maxes(Nums),
            RMaxs = compute_right_maxes(Nums),
            
            % Iterate through all elements using recursion and the precalculated max arrays
            solve_helper(Nums, LMaxs, RMaxs, []).

% Recursive helper to check validity
solve_helper([], _, _, Acc) ->
    lists:reverse(Acc);
solve_helper([H | T], [LMax | TLMaxs], [RMax | TRMaxs], Acc) ->
    Index = length(Nums) - length([H | T]), % Calculate current index i
    
    % Check boundary conditions (first or last element)
    IsFirst = Index = 0,
    IsLast = Index = length(Nums) - 1,

    % Check core conditions for middle elements
    IsGreaterThanLeft = H > LMax,
    IsGreaterThanRight = H > RMax,
    
    IsValid = IsFirst ; IsLast ; (IsGreaterThanLeft ; IsGreaterThanRight),
    
    NewAcc = if IsValid -> [H | Acc] ; Acc,
    
    solve_helper(T, TLMaxs, TRMaxs, NewAcc).

% We need to handle the length context for solve_helper to work correctly, 
% as length(Nums) is not available inside the helper scope unless passed or redefined.
% Let's restructure the iteration using a loop equivalent where N is known.

solve(Nums) when is_list(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    case N of
        0 -> [];
        1 -> Nums;
        _ ->
            LMaxs = compute_left_maxes(Nums),
            RMaxs = compute_right_maxes(Nums),
            
            % Use fold/recursion with index tracking to maintain context
            valid_elements =
                lists:foldl(fun(Acc, {i, H}) ->
                    Index = i,
                    
                    IsFirst = Index = 0,
                    IsLast = Index = N - 1,
                    
                    % LMaxs and RMaxs are 0-indexed parallel arrays
                    LMax_i = LMaxs !! Index,
                    RMax_i = RMaxs !! Index,

                    IsGreaterThanLeft = H > LMax_i,
                    IsGreaterThanRight = H > RMax_i,
                    
                    IsValid = IsFirst ; IsLast ; (IsGreaterThanLeft ; IsGreaterThanRight),
                    
                    if IsValid -> [H | Acc] ; Acc
                end, [])
                ,{Idx, Val} ||
            {Idx, Val} <- lists:zip(lists:seq(0, N-1), Nums),
            
            lists:reverse(valid_elements).

% Since Erlang list indexing (!!) is O(N), using fold/zip is preferred over indexing if possible, 
% but given the need to know the index for boundary checking, this indexed iteration is cleanest.
% The use of !! here is fine given N <= 100.

solve(Nums) ->
    case length(Nums) of
        0 -> [];
        1 -> Nums;
        N ->
            % 1. Calculate LMax: LMax[i] = max(Nums[0]...Nums[i-1]). LMax[0] = 0 (or a value smaller than any input).
            % lists:scanl(fun(acc, x) -> max(acc, x) end, 0, Nums) produces a list of length N+1, where the i-th element is the max of the prefix of length i.
            LMax_full = lists:scanl(fun(acc, X) -> max(acc, X) end, 0, Nums),
            % We need LMax[i], which is the max *before* index i. This corresponds to the i-th element of LMax_full.
            LMax = lists:sublist(LMax_full, 0, N),

            % 2. Calculate RMax: RMax[i] = max(Nums[i+1]...Nums[N-1]). RMax[N-1] = 0.
            Nums_rev = lists:reverse(Nums),
            % Calculate prefix maxes on reversed list
            S_rev_full = lists:scanl(fun(acc, X) -> max(acc, X) end, 0, Nums_rev),
            % S_rev_full has length N+1. s_i is max of first i elements of Nums_rev.
            % We need RMax[i] = s_{N-i-1}. (If we want 0-based indexing for RMax).
            % Let's adjust indices carefully.
            % RMax[N-1] = 0.
            % RMax[N-2] = max(Nums[N-1]) = s_1 (where s_1 is the first element of S_rev_full after the initial 0).
            % RMax[0] = max(Nums[1]...Nums[N-1]) = s_{N-1}.

            % We take S_rev_full[1] through S_rev_full[N-1], reverse this, and append 0.
            RMax_list = lists:reverse(lists:sublist(S_rev_full, 1, N)),
            RMax = RMax_list ++ [0],

            % 3. Filter elements
            Effective_Elements = [
                X || {X, I} <- zip(Nums, [0..N-1]),
                (I =:= 0; I =:= N-1) -> true; % First or last element is always effective
                (X > LMax[I] ; X > RMax[I]) -> true
            ],
            Effective_Elements.
```

calc_lmax(Nums) ->
    % Calculates the strict maximum encountered before each element.
    % Returns a list [m0, m1, ..., mN] where mi = max(Nums[0]...Nums[i-1]). m0 is 0.
    calculate_lmax_acc(Nums, 0, []).

calculate_lmax_acc([], _, Acc) ->
    lists:reverse(Acc).
calculate_lmax_acc([H|T], CurrentMax, Acc) ->
    % CurrentMax is the max before H (m_i)
    NewMax = max(CurrentMax, H),
    % Prepend m_i to the accumulator
    calculate_lmax_acc(T, NewMax, [CurrentMax | Acc]).

calc_rmax(Nums) ->
    % Calculates the strict maximum encountered after each element.
    % Returns a list [r0, r1, ..., rN] where ri = max(Nums[i+1]...Nums[N-1]). rN-1 is 0.
    lists:reverse(calc_rmax_acc(lists:reverse(Nums), 0, [])).

% Helper for RMax: Processes the list in reverse order.
% H is N_i, CurrentMax is max(N_{i+1}...N_{N-1})
calc_rmax_acc([], _, Acc) ->
    Acc.
calc_rmax_acc([H|T], CurrentMax, Acc) ->
    NewMax = max(CurrentMax, H),
    % Prepend the boundary max corresponding to H (r_i)
    calc_rmax_acc(T, NewMax, [CurrentMax | Acc]).

% Helper function to recursively filter valid elements
% N is the total length of the array, passed implicitly via structure matching in solve/1
filter_valid_helper([], _, _, _, Acc) -> Acc.
filter_valid_helper([H|T], [LM|LMT], [RM|RMT], I, Acc) ->
    % Check validity condition
    Valid =
        case I of
            0 -> true; % First element always valid
            _ ->
                % Check if H > LM OR H > RM
                (H > LM) or (H > RM)
        end,

    NewAcc = if Valid -> [H | Acc]; true -> Acc end,
    NextI = I + 1,
    filter_valid_helper(T, LMT, RMT, NextI, NewAcc).


solve(Nums) ->
    case length(Nums) of
        0 -> []
        1 -> Nums;
        N ->
            LMax = calc_lmax(Nums),
            RMax = calc_rmax(Nums),
            % Start filtering from index 0. We rely on the boundary conditions (I=0 and I=N-1)
            % within filter_valid_helper to handle the start/end elements correctly.
            I = 0,
            Result = filter_valid_helper(Nums, LMax, RMax, I, []),
